query_log.query: 2026-03-14 Reuters NY market summary dollar yen oil Brent WTI 10-year yield Japan
2026-03-13 TDnet 業績予想 修正 配当 自己株 site:minkabu.jp
2026-03-13 Reuters 来週の東京株式市場 原油 イラン FOMC 日銀

snapshot: Reuters' NIYcm1 quote page showed Nikkei futures at 53,765 as of March 14, down 855, leaving Monday's index tone soft.
All three major U.S. indices closed lower on Friday as oil volatility kept inflation concerns alive.
Brent settled at $103.14 and WTI at $98.71, keeping upstream energy in relative-strength territory.
USD/JPY ended at 159.67 and the U.S. 10-year yield was 4.283%, a mix of exporter support and intervention risk for Japan.
The week ahead includes the FOMC, the BOJ meeting, Nvidia's event and Micron's earnings, so semiconductors may turn event-driven again.

drivers.driver: Oil above $100, a weak yen and soft index futures
drivers.why_today: With Brent in the $103 area, USD/JPY in the upper 159s and Nikkei futures at 53,765, the index backdrop stays soft while first-order upstream beneficiaries can still outperform.

drivers.driver: A cluster of Friday after-close revisions, dividends and capital actions
drivers.why_today: North Sand, Sanno, Hayashikane Sangyo, OVAL, Terilogy Holdings and OHARA all posted disclosures that deserve priority checks at Monday's open.

headline_inventory.headline: Reuters' NIYcm1 quote page showed Nikkei futures at 53,765 as of March 14, down 855 from the prior session
New York market summary: USD/JPY 159.67, Brent 103.14, WTI 98.71, U.S. 10-year yield 4.283%, and U.S. equities finished lower
Reuters expects next week's Tokyo market to stay driven by Iran and oil, with a projected Nikkei range of 52,000-56,000
Finance minister Katayama said Japan stands ready to respond to sharp market swings, keeping intervention risk alive alongside yen weakness
North Sand released its FY2026/1 earnings statement
Hayashikane Sangyo raised full-year guidance and also lifted its dividend outlook
OVAL disclosed the cancellation of 5.18 million treasury shares
Terilogy Holdings revised earnings guidance upward

watchlist.name: INPEX
watchlist.why_today: With Brent in the $103 area, USD/JPY in the upper 159s and soft index futures, upstream energy is the first place to monitor on the long side into Monday's open.
watchpoints: Can it clearly outperform the Nikkei and TOPIX right after the opening print
Do Brent above $100 and USD/JPY in the 159s hold through Tokyo hours
Does sector buying stay concentrated in mining and upstream energy
falsifiers: Weekend de-escalation in the Middle East pushes oil sharply lower
Intervention fears or lower yields trigger a fast yen rebound
watchlist.red_team: Higher oil is also a broad risk-off input, so even resource names can get dragged lower in a hard index selloff.

sources.title: ＮＹ市場サマリー（13日）ドル上昇、株続落　利回りまちまち
落ち着きどころ探る展開、中東情勢や原油動向を注視＝来週の東京株式市場
午前の日経平均は続落、一時1100円超安　中東情勢や米株安嫌気

watchlist.name: North Sand
watchlist.why_today: It released a stronger profit plan together with a dividend-policy update and a dividend outlook, making it one of the cleaner Friday after-close follow-through candidates.
watchpoints: Can the stock avoid slipping below its opening print after a likely gap-up
Do investors reward both the growth outlook and the dividend-policy change
Does volume stay active enough for a second leg rather than a one-print pop
falsifiers: Profit-taking hits early because this is still a smaller-cap name
The dividend angle is treated as a one-year event rather than a durable reset
watchlist.red_team: The disclosures are strong, but smaller-cap setups are prone to gap-and-fade behavior.

sources.title: ノースサンド(446A) 2026年１月期決算短信〔日本基準〕（非連結）

watchlist.name: Sanno
watchlist.why_today: FY2026/7 guidance was revised upward to an 80% profit-growth outlook, putting a clear number-driven catalyst on the board.
watchpoints: Can buyers keep stepping in even if the stock opens well above Friday's cash close
Does the market keep re-rating the name against the size of the revision
Does turnover stay alive after the first burst
falsifiers: Thin turnover produces volatility without real follow-through
The revision is treated as too one-off in nature
watchlist.red_team: The earnings signal is clean, but thinner order books can reverse sharply once the first chase is over.

sources.title: 山王(3441) 2026年7月期連結業績予想の修正に関するお知らせ
山王、今期経常を一転80％増益に上方修正・19期ぶり最高益更新へ

watchlist.name: Hayashikane Sangyo
watchlist.why_today: It moved back to a profit-growth outlook and also lifted the dividend plan, a combination that often gets a clean Monday morning read.
watchpoints: Which matters more at the open, the earnings revision or the larger dividend
Can the stock absorb selling pressure during the first 30 minutes
Does money rotate into lower-priced value names alongside it
falsifiers: The dividend increase is seen as non-recurring
watchlist.red_team: The setup is attractive, but the name can still trade like a short-term lower-priced momentum move.

sources.title: 林兼(2286) 連結業績予想の修正ならびに配当予想の修正（増配）に関するお知らせ

watchlist.name: OVAL
watchlist.why_today: The company disclosed cancellation of 5.18 million treasury shares, large enough to make supply improvement a real open-check item.
watchpoints: Can it press higher on real turnover after the open
Does the cancellation start to be valued as more than a one-day event
Does money keep rotating into capital-action names
falsifiers: The move turns into a simple sell-the-news reaction
Broader market weakness overwhelms the capital-policy story
watchlist.red_team: The capital action is clear, but without a parallel earnings hook the follow-through can fade.

sources.title: オーバル(7727) 自己株式の消却に関するお知らせ
オーバルが自社株５１８万株を消却へ

watchlist.name: Terilogy Holdings
watchlist.why_today: It raised full-year ordinary-profit guidance by 45%, creating a direct earnings-driven small-cap catalyst for Monday morning.
watchpoints: Can it absorb early selling even if it gaps higher
Does the market reward the cleaner profit outlook without hesitation
Does small-cap growth interest return alongside the name
falsifiers: Risk-off trading across small caps dominates
Profit-taking wins despite the upward revision
watchlist.red_team: Even good disclosures can become one-session volatility trades if liquidity stays one-sided.

sources.title: テリロジHD(5133) 業績予想の修正に関するお知らせ
テリロジＨＤ、今期経常を45％上方修正・5期ぶり最高益更新へ

watchlist.name: OHARA
watchlist.why_today: It revised FY2026/10 guidance upward after the close, making it a straightforward post-earnings reassessment candidate into Monday's open.
watchpoints: Does the stock find a clean post-open base after the guidance revision
Does the market prioritize the full-year revision over the weak first quarter
Does the broader bid for small and mid-cap revisions stay alive
falsifiers: Investors focus on the weaker first quarter instead
The full-year revision is treated as already expected
watchlist.red_team: The quarter itself was not especially strong, so the full-year revision may not be enough on its own.

deep_dive.title: INPEX: In a weak tape, first check whether the first-order oil beneficiary still leads

lines: If Brent stays above $100 and USD/JPY remains in the upper 159s, upstream energy should remain Japan's clearest first-order beneficiary into Monday's open.
When the index is under pressure, large-cap names tied directly to external variables tend to price faster than more narrative-driven stories.
Friday's U.S. session closed lower on oil and inflation worries, and Reuters expects Tokyo next week to stay sensitive to Iran and crude.
In Friday morning Tokyo trading, mining and oil & coal already ranked among the stronger sectors, showing the market has started to rotate that way.
Can INPEX clearly outperform the Nikkei and TOPIX right after the opening print.
Do Brent above $100 and USD/JPY in the 159s remain intact during Tokyo hours.
Weekend de-escalation in the Middle East causes oil to cool quickly.
Intervention fears or lower yields drive a meaningful yen rebound and remove the external tailwind.
Higher oil is still a macro headwind for Japan overall, so resource shares can get caught in broader de-risking.
With the FOMC and BOJ meetings ahead, position-cutting may cap upside even if relative strength holds.

no_trade_conditions: If weekend Middle East headlines materially change the oil premise before Tokyo opens
If Nikkei futures recover most of Friday's late weakness and the trade shifts from relative longs to a broad index rebound
If USD/JPY reverses sharply on intervention or policy shocks and the weaker-yen premise breaks
If Friday after-close revision names fail to hold their opening levels and follow-through buying does not appear
